Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Chris Hersom, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Frederick Rudy.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Economic Damages in the amount of $50,000.00
Non-Economic Damages in the amount of $300,000.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $350,000.00
Counsel for Plaintiff to submit Judgment for signature within five days.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Frederick Rudy

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Frederick Rudy

RULING:
DEFENDANT HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Crosby and Mr. Crum are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
